Title: N6-methyladenosine modifications stabilize phosphate starvation response–related mRNAs in plant adaptation to nutrient-deficient stress

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: artificial miRNA knockdown (amiR-mta); LC-MS/MS quantification of global m6A; RNA-seq; transcriptome-wide RNA decay assay; RT-qPCR; physiological Pi and root assays; chemical inhibition with STM2457
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core m6A writer; MTA protein abundance was not obviously changed by Pi starvation or phr1 phl1 mutation; PHR1 interacts with MTA in nuclei under -P; knockdown reduced global m6A to ~40% of WT and attenuated systemic PSR signaling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth inhibition under +P and worsened under -P; reduced biomass; shorter primary roots; reduced shoot Pi concentration
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: m6A RNA methyltransferase writer complex component; METTL3 ortholog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in seedlings; transcript reduced to ~25% of WT in amiR-mta; functional under +P and -P conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate homeostasis and systemic phosphate starvation response signaling by maintaining m6A methylation and stabilizing PSR-related mRNAs
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; transcriptome-wide RNA decay RNA-seq; PAS-seq2; GSA: CRA016391
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating m6A writer activity could tune phosphate starvation adaptation, but partial suppression causes growth defects and reduced Pi accumulation, so precise or conditional engineering would be required
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pi deficiency (-P, 6.25 uM Pi); STM2457 m6A writer inhibitor; cordycepin for RNA decay assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTA is required for normal global m6A levels in Arabidopsis seedlings. 2. Knockdown of MTA lowers shoot Pi concentration and attenuates systemic PSR gene expression under Pi starvation. 3. Reduced MTA compromises stabilization of PSR-related transcripts, including PHR1, under -P.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate is an essential mineral nutrient required for plant growth, photosynthesis, nucleic acids, and essential metabolite biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; nuclear Co-IP with PHR1 under -P
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: m6A writer complex component interacting with PHR1 in nuclei under Pi deficiency; protein level in nuclei comparable between +P and -P according to study
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: m6A RNA methyltransferase writer complex component; METTL14 ortholog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in seedlings under +P and -P; transcript abundance not greatly altered by Pi deficiency or phr1 phl1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associates with PHR1 and likely contributes to Pi starvation-induced m6A deposition on PSR-related transcripts
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering Pi-starvation epitranscriptomic responses, but direct phenotypic validation was not performed here
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTB interacts with PHR1 in Y2H, BiFC, and nuclear Co-IP assays under -P. 2. MTB is implicated in PHR-mediated induction of m6A during Pi starvation. 3. Its transcript/protein abundance was not strongly changed by Pi status, suggesting regulation through interaction rather than expression.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate is essential for plant nutrient homeostasis and stress adaptation

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phr1 phl1 mutant m6A-seq; Y2H; BiFC; nuclear Co-IP; RT-qPCR; target-specific RNA decay; complementation with native-promoter PHR1-WT and 3'UTR m6A-loci mutant transgenes; anthocyanin and Pi-response phenotyping
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Master PSR regulator whose own transcript carries Pi starvation-induced m6A in the 3'UTR; m6A-site mutagenesis reduced transcript half-life and only partially complemented phr1; acts in positive feedback loop with m6A machinery
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation of PHR1 m6A sites reduced anthocyanin accumulation under -P and weakened PSI gene induction
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GARP transcription factor with MYB-like DNA-binding domain and coiled-coil domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in seedlings; steady-state transcript moderately reduced in amiR-mta under -P; functional under Pi starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate starvation response signaling and Pi homeostasis; m6A modification stabilizes PHR1 mRNA, facilitating downstream PSI gene expression
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; transcriptome-wide RNA decay RNA-seq; PAS-seq2; GSA: CRA016391
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing PHR1 transcript stability via native 3'UTR m6A sites may improve phosphate-deficiency adaptation; editing should preserve functional m6A motifs
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PPHR1 promoter in complementation constructs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pi deficiency (-P); cordycepin for decay assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHR1 is a central PSR factor whose transcript gains m6A under Pi starvation. 2. PHR1 mediates much of the global PSI-m6A response, likely through interaction with MTA/MTB rather than by transcriptional induction of writer genes. 3. m6A in the PHR1 3'UTR stabilizes PHR1 mRNA and is required for full PSI signaling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seedlings under Pi starvation response conditions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate is required for growth and metabolism; PSR helps plants adapt to phosphate-deficient stress

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phr1 phl1 double mutant m6A-seq comparison with WT
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHR1 homolog acting with PHR1 to mediate global PSI-m6A changes under Pi starvation; no direct interaction assays with MTA/MTB reported here
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss together with PHR1 diminished PSI-m6A induction under -P
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in seedlings under Pi starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with PHR1 to induction of Pi starvation-associated m6A modifications and PSR signaling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with PHR1 for tuning PSR-associated epitranscriptomic regulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of PHL1 together with PHR1 strongly reduces global PSI-m6A induction. 2. PHL1 contributes to PHR-mediated control of epitranscriptomic PSR responses under Pi starvation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phr1 phl1 double mutant m6A-seq; Y2H; BiFC; nuclear Co-IP; RT-qPCR; target-specific RNA decay; complementation with native-promoter PHR1-WT and 3'UTR m6A-loci mutant transgenes; anthocyanin phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Master regulator of systemic phosphate starvation response; its transcript is itself m6A modified under -P and stabilized by this modification, forming a positive feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss or impaired m6A-dependent stabilization weakens PSI signaling and anthocyanin accumulation under Pi deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GARP transcription factor; MYB-like DNA-binding domain; coiled-coil domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in seedlings under Pi starvation; transcript level moderately reduced in amiR-mta under -P
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Pi starvation response signaling and Pi homeostasis; mediates Pi starvation-induced m6A deposition and requires m6A for its own transcript stability
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of systemic PSR and major driver of Pi starvation-induced m6A remodeling on PSR-related mRNAs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; transcriptome-wide RNA decay RNA-seq; PAS-seq2; GSA: CRA016391
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preserving or enhancing native PHR1 m6A-dependent transcript stability may strengthen phosphate-deficiency adaptation; avoid editing that disrupts functional 3'UTR m6A motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Systemic phosphate starvation response signaling and Pi homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: m6A RNA modification in the 3'UTR stabilizes PHR1 transcript; six of seven putative sites validated/abolished by mutagenesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PHF1; PHO1; PHT1;1/PT1; ACP5; broader PSI gene set
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates systemic PSR-signaling genes; indirectly associated with stabilization of some PSD transcripts through PHR-mediated PSI-m6A feedback
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of PSI genes; interaction-mediated recruitment/association with m6A writers to promote epitranscriptomic regulation; positive feedback via mRNA stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PPHR1 promoter in complementation constructs
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop: Pi starvation induces PHR-mediated PSI-m6A, and m6A stabilizes PHR1 mRNA to reinforce PSI signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pi deficiency (-P); cordycepin for RNA decay measurements
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHR1 is required for much of the Pi starvation-induced m6A increase across the transcriptome. 2. PHR1 physically interacts with MTA and MTB in nuclei under -P, suggesting a noncanonical epitranscriptomic regulatory role. 3. m6A sites in the PHR1 3'UTR stabilize its mRNA and are required for full downstream PSI signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seedlings, especially under Pi starvation response conditions
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate supports fundamental metabolism and growth; PSR improves adaptation to Pi deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimers/heterodimers with PHL proteins in PSR; interacts with m6A writer components MTA and MTB

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phr1 phl1 double mutant m6A-seq and RNA-seq under +P and -P
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHR1-like family member acting with PHR1 to mediate Pi starvation-induced m6A changes; specific direct biochemical interaction with writers not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with PHR1 reduces PSI-m6A remodeling and PSR output
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in seedlings during Pi starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports PHR-dependent induction of Pi starvation-associated m6A modifications and PSR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-central regulator with PHR1 for systemic PSR and PSI-m6A induction under Pi deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be considered with PHR1 for engineering robust phosphate starvation responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with PHR1 in PSR gene regulation and PSI-m6A induction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation and indirect epitranscriptomic control inferred from double mutant effects
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Contributes to PHR-mediated PSI-m6A feedback network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHL1 cooperates with PHR1 to mediate most Pi starvation-induced m6A hypermethylation events. 2. The phr1 phl1 mutant loses much of the -P-dependent m6A induction, indicating central regulatory function.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PHR family dimerization with PHR1 supported in background knowledge cited by paper
